description: Comprehensive Practice Worksheet Database for Vector Calculus (18 types, Dawkins Ch 11-12). Basic: Vector from Points, Position Vector, Vector Arithmetic, Unit Vector, Parallel Vectors, Vector Endpoint. Medium: Dot Product, Angle Between Vectors, Orthogonal/Parallel/Neither, Vector Projection, Direction Cosines, Cross Product. Hard: Vector Function Domain, Vector Function Graph, Line Segment Equation, Vector Function Limit, Vector Function Derivative, Vector Function Integral.

questions.question_text: Find the vector equation of the line segment from \( P(-2, 3) \) to \( Q(3, -1) \).
Find the unit vector in the direction of \( \mathbf{v} = \langle 2, -1 \rangle \).
If \( \|\mathbf{a}\| = 4 \), \( \|\mathbf{b}\| = 2 \), and the angle between them is \( \theta = \frac{\pi}{3} \), find \( \mathbf{a} \cdot \mathbf{b} \).
Evaluate \( \lim_{t \to 2} \mathbf{r}(t) \) where \( \mathbf{r}(t) = \langle e^{- 3 t}, - 3 t^{2} + 5 t + 1, - 2 t^{2} + 5 t - 1 \rangle \).
A vector \( \mathbf{v} = \langle -1, 1 \rangle \) starts at the point \( P(-3, -3) \). Find the terminal point.
Find the vector equation of the line segment from \( P(2, 1, 2) \) to \( Q(2, -1, -1) \).
Find the angle between \( \mathbf{a} = \langle 2, 1 \rangle \) and \( \mathbf{b} = \langle 1, 2 \rangle \).
Identify the type of curve traced by \( \mathbf{r}(t) = \langle 2 t + 3, t - 1, t + 1 \rangle \).
questions.answer_latex: \text{The curve is a line}, \; \text{passing through } (3, -1, 1) \text{ with direction } \langle 2, 1, 1 \rangle
questions.answer_plain: The curve is a line, passing through (3, -1, 1) with direction <2, 1, 1>
